name: Bentley Systems iTwin Platform API
what_it_does: Bentley Systems iTwin Platform REST API for digital twin, BIM, and infrastructure engineering automation. Enables AI agents to manage infrastructure digital twin data and model management for engineering asset lifecycle, handle BIM (Building Information Modeling) model access and versioning for construction and operations, access geospatial and 3D model data for infrastructure visualization and analysis, retrieve project delivery and engineering change management data for capital project management, manage reality capture and scan-to-BIM workflow for infrastructure documentation, handle asset performance monitoring and IoT sensor integration for operational digital twins, access civil engineering and structural analysis data for engineering automation, retrieve regulatory compliance and permit documentation for infrastructure projects, manage multi-discipline coordination and clash detection for engineering review workflow, and integrate Bentley iTwin data with GIS, ERP, and operations management platforms.
use_cases: Accessing infrastructure digital twins from engineering agents
Managing BIM models from capital project agents
Monitoring asset performance from operations agents
Coordinating engineering disciplines from project delivery agents
not_for: Simple CAD without BIM/digital twin engineering context
Commercial building without infrastructure (roads, bridges, utilities) focus
Small firms without enterprise infrastructure engineering scale
best_when: An infrastructure owner, engineering firm, or EPC contractor using Bentley iTwin Platform wants AI agents to automate digital twin management, BIM data access, asset performance monitoring, and engineering project delivery.
avoid_when: ENGINEERING SAFETY RISK: Bentley digital twin data directly informs infrastructure safety decisions — automated structural analysis result interpretation without licensed engineer review creates liability for infrastructure safety decisions. BIM model version management automation must maintain audit trail for design change documentation per ISO 19650 BIM management standards. iTwin platform permissions are complex; automated access without proper project-level permission management creates unauthorized data access.

auth.notes: Bentley iTwin uses OAuth 2.0. Developer portal at developer.bentley.com. Webhooks for iTwin events. REST API with JSON. Exton, Pennsylvania HQ. Public (BSY NASDAQ). Founded 1984 by brothers Greg and Barry Bentley. Bentley iTwin is the digital twin platform (BIM, infrastructure). 4,000+ infrastructure engineering customers. Strong in roads, bridges, rail, utilities, and industrial. Competes with Autodesk Construction Cloud and Trimble for engineering and construction technology platform.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer tier with limited iTwin storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Subscription by iTwin storage and API consumption; enterprise contracts
pricing.notes: Exton, Pennsylvania. Bentley Systems (BSY NASDAQ). Founded 1984. 4,000+ infrastructure customers. iTwin Platform for digital twins. Competes with Autodesk AEC Collection and Trimble for infrastructure engineering software.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; consumption-based pricing

known_agent_gotchas: ENGINEERING SAFETY RISK: Infrastructure digital twin structural and safety analysis must be reviewed by licensed Professional Engineer before safety-critical decisions; automated decision-making on structural data creates PE liability

ISO 19650 BIM management — BIM model version management must comply with ISO 19650 information management standards for infrastructure projects; automated versioning without proper CDE (Common Data Environment) workflow creates BIM management non-compliance
iTwin permission complexity — iTwin project permissions are granular (project, iModel, context); automated access without proper RBAC configuration creates unauthorized data access
3D model data volume — BIM and point cloud data can be very large (GBs); automated iTwin data queries for large infrastructure models create bandwidth and latency issues

Engineering change management — automated BIM model updates must follow formal engineering change process for regulated infrastructure (bridges, nuclear, etc.); unauthorized model changes create safety and regulatory issues
Digital twin IoT latency — real-time digital twin monitoring via IoT sensor integration has data ingestion latency; automation workflows must account for sensor data lag
agent_readiness.security_notes: Infrastructure digital twin. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Engineering and infrastructure model data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Infrastructure digital twin. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Engineering model data.
